learning_outcomes.description: Identify and explain key principles of public law, including their meaning, historical development, grounding in the Australian Constitution, and relationship to one another.
Identify the institutions of government and constitutional actors, and explain their basic public law foundations, composition, roles and powers.
Apply principles of public law to critically analyse institutional power in the Australian constitutional system.
Independently conduct and communicate research in public law, applying appropriate principle and methods.
Demonstrate case analysis and statutory interpretation in a public law context.
Develop reasoned arguments to support recommendations for reform.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment task is designed to develop students' critical reading skills. The ability to read, understand and engage deeply and critically with texts is foundational to legal practice and many other professional endeavours.

The assessment will also help students to prepare for the in-semester examination, as it will require students to read material that will be assessed in the in-semester examination.

Students will be required to annotate an assigned reading relating to a topic from the earlier parts of the course. Students will also be required to write a short reflection on their experience of critical reading. The reflection will be subject to a word limit. Any words that exceed the word limit will not be read or marked.

More information will be supplied in announcements and guidelines via the course website.

The assessment is due on Tuesday 18 August (Week 4) at 2pm.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The assignment must be submitted electronically via the assessment submission link on the course Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
21/09/2026 - 25/09/2026

Week 9 - Date and time to be confirmed.
assessments.task_description:
This assessment will be an in-person, handwritten examination. The exam will require students to demonstrate their understanding of early parts of the course through a critical engagement with several pre-assigned readings, including the reading covered by the critical reading exercise.

More information will be supplied in announcements and guidelines via the course website.

The exam will be held on campus in Week 9. Date and time to be confirmed.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description: This assessment will be an in-person, handwritten examination held during the end-of-semester exam period. Before the end of the course, students will be given further information about the topics which are examinable and the exam format.
